content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660           322-5901           322-6441           322-6444

May 23, 1983


Steven H. Kaufmann
Deputy Attorney General
1515 K Street, Suite 511
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Advice No. A-83-130

Dear Mr. Kaufmann:

You have asked for advice as to whether Coastal Commissioner
Roger D. Slates must disqualify himself from decisions which
might have a material financial effect on Chevron USA, Inc. You
stated that Commissioner Slates is managing partner of, and owns
a 50% interest in, Seacliff Realty Company in Huntington Beach,
California. The other 50% of Seacliff Realty is owned by a
corporation (Seacliff Corporation) which is a wholly owned
subsidiary of the Huntington Beach Company. Chevron USA, Inc.,
owns 60% of the Huntington Beach Company.

The Political Reform Act, Government Code Sections 87100,
87103, requires a public official to disqualify himself from
making or participating in decisions which will foreseeably have
a material financial effect on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth more than
one thousand dollars ($1,000).

(b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth more than
one thousand dollars ($1,000).

(c) Any source of income, other than loans by a
commercial lending institution in the regular course of
business on terms available to the public without
regard to official status, aggregating two hundred

Steven H. Kaufmann
May 23, 1983
Page 2


     fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official within
12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.

     (d)  Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

     On the basis of the facts provided in your advice request
and the FPPC's advice and opinions under Sections 87100 and
87103, it does not appear that Commissioner Slates has any
potential conflict with respect to Chevron, USA.  He does not
have an investment interest in Chevron USA, nor does he have any
investment in Chevron's real estate.  Chevron USA is not a
source of income to Commissioner Slates by virtue of its
indirect ownership interest in Seacliff Corporation.
Commissioner Slates holds no position of employment or
management in Chevron USA.  Therefore, Commissioner Slates will
not have to disqualify himself from Coastal Commission decisions
affecting Chevron USA.

     I would like to point out, however, that the Commission is
expected to issue an opinion (opinion requested by Steven Nord)
this summer concerning the potential for conflicts of interest
arising from limited partnership relations.  While I do not
think it likely that the opinion issued to Mr. Nord will have
any effect on our advice to Commissioner Slates, it is possible
that the opinion will touch on situations similar to that which
you have described in your advice request.  If you would like to
be kept informed of the progress of the Nord opinion, I suggest
you contact Robert E. Leidigh, who is the attorney in this
office who will be handling that matter.

                                   Very truly yours,

                                   [signature]
                                   Barbara A. Milman
                                   Chief
                                   Legal Division

BAM:plh

State of California                                    Department of Justice

Memorandum

To   :  Janis McLean, Legal Counsel              Date :  May 12, 1983
       Fair Political Practices Commission
       1100 K Street, P.O. Box 807
       Sacramento, CA  95804
                                                 File No.:


From :  Office of the Attorney General - Steven H. Kaufmann, Deputy Attorney General
        LOS ANGELES

Subject:  Request for Advice: Coastal
         Commissioner Roger D. Slates


        Through this office, Coastal Commissioner Roger D.
Slates requests your advice concerning whether he would have any
conflict of interest in voting on matters before the Coastal
Commission involving Chevron USA, Inc. The question arises
because the Coastal Commission, from time to time, is called upon
to review and vote on various oil and gas exploration and
development activities proposed by Chevron USA, Inc., in the
coastal zone and in the Outer Continental Shelf off California.

        To assist you, Commissioner Slates has provided us with
the following factual background. He is a 50% owner and
managing partner of Seacliff Realty Company, in Huntington Beach,
California. Seacliff Realty Company is in the business of
handling real estate matters in the City of Huntington Beach and
Orange County.

        Seacliff Corporation is the other partner of Seacliff
Realty Company. Seacliff Corporation is a wholly owned
subsidiary of the Huntington Beach Company, which is a land
development and holding company. Among other things, it is the
owner of approximately seven to eight oil wells within the City
of Huntington Beach. None of the directors or officers of
Seacliff Corporation sit as directors or officers of the
Huntington Beach Company.

        Chevron USA, Inc., owns 60% of the Huntington Beach
Company. Neither Commissioner Slates, Seacliff Corporation,
Seacliff Realty Company, nor the Huntington Beach Company have
any ownership interest in Chevron USA, Inc. Neither do they
derive any income or direct material financial benefit from any
of Chevron's oil and gas exploration and development activities
in California or in the Outer Continental Shelf off California.

Janis McLean
Page 2
May 12, 1983


Your advice should be forwarded directly to Roger D.
Slates at the address indicated below.

Please let us know if there is anything further we can
do to assist you in your determination.


[signature]
STEVEN H. KAUFMANN
Deputy Attorney General

SHK:qh

cc: Roger Slates
    1701 Main Street
    Huntington Beach, CA  92648

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

May 17, 1983

Steven H. Kaufmann
Deputy Attorney General
3580 Wilshire Blvd.
Los Angeles, CA 90010

Re: A-83-130

Dear Mr. Kaufmann:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to me. If you have any
questions about your advice request, please contact me
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief
Legal Division

BAM:plb
Enclosure

sections.question: Whether Coastal Commissioner Roger D. Slates must disqualify himself from decisions which might have a material financial effect on Chevron USA, Inc., given his status as managing partner and 50% owner of Seacliff Realty Company, whose other 50% partner (Seacliff Corporation) is a wholly owned subsidiary of Huntington Beach Company, which is 60% owned by Chevron USA, Inc.
sections.conclusion: On the basis of the facts provided in your advice request and the FPPC's advice and opinions under Sections 87100 and 87103, it does not appear that Commissioner Slates has any potential conflict with respect to Chevron, USA. He does not have an investment interest in Chevron USA, nor does he have any investment in Chevron's real estate. Chevron USA is not a source of income to Commissioner Slates by virtue of its indirect ownership interest in Seacliff Corporation. Commissioner Slates holds no position of employment or management in Chevron USA. Therefore, Commissioner Slates will not have to disqualify himself from Coastal Commission decisions affecting Chevron USA.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a Coastal Commissioner disqualify himself from voting on matters involving Chevron USA when he owns 50% of a realty company whose other 50% partner is a subsidiary of a company 60% owned by Chevron?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Commissioner Slates does not have a disqualifying conflict of interest with respect to Chevron USA decisions. Although he owns 50% of Seacliff Realty, the indirect connection to Chevron through multiple corporate layers does not create a material financial effect under Political Reform Act Sections 87100 and 87103.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request memo (May 12, 1983) and FPPC response letter (May 23, 1983). Both sections clearly present the question and the FPPC's formal conclusion. Minor text degradation in memorandum portion did not significantly impact extraction.

QUESTION: Whether Coastal Commissioner Roger D. Slates must disqualify himself from decisions which might have a material financial effect on Chevron USA, Inc., given his status as managing partner and 50% owner of Seacliff Realty Company, whose other 50% partner (Seacliff Corporation) is a wholly owned subsidiary of Huntington Beach Company, which is 60% owned by Chevron USA, Inc.

CONCLUSION: On the basis of the facts provided in your advice request and the FPPC's advice and opinions under Sections 87100 and 87103, it does not appear that Commissioner Slates has any potential conflict with respect to Chevron, USA. He does not have an investment interest in Chevron USA, nor does he have any investment in Chevron's real estate. Chevron USA is not a source of income to Commissioner Slates by virtue of its indirect ownership interest in Seacliff Corporation. Commissioner Slates holds no position of employment or management in Chevron USA. Therefore, Commissioner Slates will not have to disqualify himself from Coastal Commission decisions affecting Chevron USA.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises that Coastal Commissioner Roger D. Slates need not disqualify himself from voting on matters involving Chevron USA, as his ownership of Seacliff Realty Company does not constitute a direct or indirect investment in Chevron USA nor create any disqualifying financial interest. The opinion analyzes the multi-layered corporate ownership structure and concludes no conflict of interest exists under Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103.
